Comment: Class is split between two professors, both aren't great, focusing on his half, no grading criteria or feedback. What he teaches your honestly better off watching a few YouTube videos. Tries to be creative with work but falls short, more frustrating than fun.

Lokesh is kind and approachable with a relaxed teaching style. We did case studies, zyBooks labs, and projects that covered the basics. I would've liked more hands-on, practical activities to make it feel more engaging. Overall, an easy and organized intro course.
Lectures are a little random but very easy intro level class
Great guy, very respected and cares for his students.

Lokesh is an AMAZING professor! He offers so many opportunities for extra credit, and his exams aren't hard at all especially if you study. I know that he has a lot on his plate to balance, but he always takes the time to get to know his students and his lectures are very easy to understand. He also does Kahoots for class sometimes.
Tags: Participation matters, EXTRA CREDIT, Amazing lectures

Comment: Lokesh is great.

One of the nicest people in the entire engineering department. He wants you to succeed but what is holding him back is his ability to actually lecture and take advantage of the time he has to teach. Tests are super easy unlike his counterpart Stephan Murrell who is an actual menace and should be banned from teaching. Overall take his class he great

Professor Lokesh cares a lot about his students and makes sure no one is failing his class. He gives a lot of grade incentives so that no one fails and is always here to help his students. He gives practice exams before the actual exams to help practice for the exams, but the questions are not the same. There are labs due weekly and some are tough.

I learned so much and work was very manageable. All around amazing class and I was able to apply skills I learned to the real world which is awesome. Lokesh is a very good lecturer and will explain confusing topics in simple terms.
He is so good
Funny class.  I look forward to it every day. He cares about his students and his tests aren't hard.

Lokesh is awesome. He cares very much about his students and makes an effort to get to know everyone despite the fact he teaches a lot of different classes. He is a gem of the engineering department and is super easy to get a hold of for help. Take him if you can.

Lokesh is a great guy! He keeps the class engaging by switching up teaching format and using Kahoots. He seems to truly care about his students and their success in the classroom. I highly recommend taking a class with him if you have the opportunity!
Tags: Hilarious, Caring, Graded by few things

Comment: Very easy class, weekly quizzes, and if you pay just enough attention you will do just fine. Professor is very understanding and flexible with tests, quizzes and homework.
Tags: Caring, Graded by few things, Accessible outside class

Comment: Possibly the best computer science professor I have had so far at this school. He knows what he's talking about and he has years of industry experience to back it up. Lectures are interesting and he explains all of the concepts very well. Take lots of notes because he tends to go fast. Weekly assignments with 4 quizzes and a short paper.
Tags: Amazing lectures, Lots of homework, Lecture heavy

Comment: One of the easiest and fun classes to do! You learn a bit of computer theory, Python and Excel. Kahoots everyday and online MCQ exams per month, a final group project at end. He explains in a very fun and enjoyable way and is very approachable and genuinely wants to help. This was the first time the class was offered so it was a bit disorganized.
Tags: Accessible outside class, Graded by few things, Caring

Comment: Enjoyed the class thoroughly. Lot of work for the group project, but worth the efforts.
Tags: Respected, Accessible outside class, Group projects

Comment: He is one of the nicest professors in the UM engineering department. Take his ECE 368 class because it will give you skills and more programming knowledge for the other upper level courses. He wants to help you reach your goals.
I asked my mom for a looped video of him saying "cash" for Christmas this year. Good at teaching. Also cute.

This particular class of his was easy and simple. The lecture slides were short and to the point. His lecturing was sometimes boring but not always. There is one homework assignment a week and a straightforward quiz every one to two weeks. There is a final group project at the end of the semester. Show up, study for the quizzes for an easy A.
Tags: Participation matters, Test heavy, Lecture heavy

Comment: Great professor. Really cares about teaching and knows how to teach. Really nice and understanding guy and tries to help you learn the material. If you see ANY class with his name on it, take it with no hesitation.

CSC314 is already quite a bit of material, especially for CS students not already familiar with the subjects, and this professor dos nothing to make the class any easier. Examples are rushed, test problems are difficult, and questions are rarely answered clearly. Would not recommend this professor for this course.
Tags: Tough Grader, GRADED BY FEW THINGS

Comment: I pretty much took this class for the easy 'A'. You have to show up to at least 80% of his classes to get full credit in attendance. Other than that, he barely gave out any homework and the midterm/final exams were pretty difficult. However, if you study his lectures you should do well on the midterm and final.
Tags: Clear grading criteria, GRADED BY FEW THINGS

Comment: Currently taking ECE118 with this man and he is the man. Super nice and more importantly knows what he is talking about. Love his accent. Easy to understand.
Nicest professor I've ever had. Cares about his students. Classes are very interactive and he wants everyone to participate. Lots of homework but they are simple and not long.
